nodes.title: Derivatives
nodes.notes:
# Derivatives

Derivatives (6.5% exam weight) covers the L1 derivatives curriculum: forward commitments (forwards, futures, FRAs, swaps), contingent claims (options, CDS), no-arbitrage pricing via cost-of-carry and replication, the binomial and Black-Scholes-Merton option pricing models, the Greeks at overview level, and canonical multi-leg option strategies. Heavy on payoff/profit diagrams, put-call parity manipulations, and recognizing when each instrument or strategy applies. Most computational questions test forward pricing, FRA settlement, swap rate derivation from the discount curve, one-period binomial valuation, and break-even analysis on multi-leg strategies.

**Exam weight (mid-point):** 6.5%
**Learning Modules:** 7
**Estimated study time:** 20-30h

children.title: Forward commitments vs contingent claims
children.notes:
# Forward commitments vs contingent claims

**LOS:** LOS 1.a
**Difficulty:** 2 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
Forward commitments (forwards, futures, swaps) obligate both parties to a future transaction at agreed terms; contingent claims (options, CDS) give one party a right exercisable only if conditions favor it. Exam tests the asymmetric vs symmetric payoff distinction and which instruments fall in each category.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Forward payoff (long) = ST − F0; Long call payoff = max(ST − X, 0)

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Forgetting that a swap is a forward commitment, not a contingent claim
- Treating futures payoff as asymmetric — it is symmetric like forwards

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- AnalystPrep CFA L1 — Derivatives Question Bank

children.title: Exchange-traded vs OTC derivatives
children.notes:
# Exchange-traded vs OTC derivatives

**LOS:** LOS 1.b
**Difficulty:** 2 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
Exchange-traded derivatives are standardized, centrally cleared, and marked-to-market daily (futures, listed options); OTC derivatives are customized bilateral contracts (forwards, swaps, FRAs, OTC options, CDS) with bilateral or central counterparty clearing post-2010 reforms. Exam asks about counterparty risk, liquidity, and customization trade-offs.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- —

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Assuming all OTC derivatives are unregulated post-Dodd-Frank — most standardized swaps now clear through CCPs
- Confusing standardization with liquidity — listed options can still be illiquid

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- AnalystPrep CFA L1 — Derivatives Question Bank

children.title: Linear vs non-linear payoffs
children.notes:
# Linear vs non-linear payoffs

**LOS:** LOS 1.a
**Difficulty:** 2 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
Linear (symmetric) payoffs scale one-for-one with the underlying — forwards, futures, swaps. Non-linear (asymmetric) payoffs cap losses or gains at zero through optionality — options, callable bonds, CDS protection. The distinction drives hedging strategy choice.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Linear payoff slope = 1; Option delta varies between 0 and 1 (call) or −1 and 0 (put)

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Treating a swap as non-linear because of variable cash flows — payoff vs rates is still linear
- Confusing non-linearity with non-monotonicity

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- AnalystPrep CFA L1 — Derivatives Question Bank

# Hedger

**LOS:** LOS 1.c
**Difficulty:** 1 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
A hedger uses derivatives to reduce exposure to an existing risk — e.g. an airline buying oil futures to lock in fuel cost. The hedger typically holds an offsetting cash-market position; derivative gains/losses neutralize cash-market losses/gains.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- —

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Confusing hedging with speculation — hedger has prior exposure being offset

children.title: Arbitrageur
children.notes:
# Arbitrageur

**LOS:** LOS 1.c
**Difficulty:** 2 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
An arbitrageur exploits price discrepancies between related instruments to lock in riskless profit (e.g. cash-and-carry between spot and futures). Arbitrage activity enforces no-arbitrage pricing relationships that derivative valuation models assume.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Arbitrage profit = positive PV with zero net investment and zero risk

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Calling a strategy "arbitrage" when it carries residual risk (statistical arb, merger arb)
- Forgetting transaction costs that may eliminate small arbitrage opportunities

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- AnalystPrep CFA L1 — Derivatives Question Bank

children.title: Functions of derivatives in capital markets
children.notes:
# Functions of derivatives in capital markets

**LOS:** LOS 1.d
**Difficulty:** 2 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
Derivatives serve six core functions: risk transfer/hedging, price discovery, market completion (synthetic exposures), operational efficiency (lower transaction cost than cash markets), allocational efficiency, and information aggregation. The exam frames these as benefits to set up later discussion of risks (leverage, counterparty, complexity).

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- —

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Listing only hedging — examiners want at least 3-4 distinct functions
- Confusing market efficiency with allocational efficiency

children.title: Forward contract definition and mechanics
children.notes:
# Forward contract definition and mechanics

**LOS:** LOS 2.a
**Difficulty:** 2 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
A forward contract is a customized OTC agreement to buy/sell an underlying at a fixed price F0 on a specified future date T. No money changes hands at inception (V0=0); settlement (cash or physical) occurs at maturity. Long benefits if ST > F0, short benefits if ST < F0.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Long forward payoff at T = ST − F0

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Assuming forwards require an upfront payment — value at inception is zero
- Confusing forward price F0 (set today, paid at T) with spot price S0

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- AnalystPrep CFA L1 — Derivatives Question Bank

# Settlement: cash settlement

**LOS:** LOS 2.b
**Difficulty:** 1 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
In cash settlement the loser pays the winner the cash difference (ST − F0) at maturity; no physical delivery occurs. Common for equity index forwards/futures and most interest rate derivatives.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Cash settlement amount (long) = ST − F0

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Forgetting that cash settlement still requires reliable price-fixing on settlement date

children.title: Counterparty risk in forwards
children.notes:
# Counterparty risk in forwards

**LOS:** LOS 2.c
**Difficulty:** 2 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
Forwards expose each side to bilateral default risk; the in-the-money party bears credit exposure equal to the contract's mark-to-market value to that side. Unlike futures, forwards lack daily margining and central clearing, so exposure can grow large between trade and maturity.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Credit exposure (long, t<T) ≈ max(Vt, 0)

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Treating forwards as risk-free until maturity — credit exposure builds during life
- Ignoring potential exposure (PFE) vs current exposure

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- AnalystPrep CFA L1 — Derivatives Question Bank

# Standardization features of futures

**LOS:** LOS 2.d
**Difficulty:** 1 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
Futures contracts standardize quality, quantity, delivery date, and delivery location — enabling exchange trading and fungibility. The exchange (CCP) becomes counterparty to both sides via novation, eliminating bilateral counterparty risk.

- —

- —

## Common pitfalls

children.title: Marking-to-market (daily settlement)
children.notes:
# Marking-to-market (daily settlement)

**LOS:** LOS 2.d
**Difficulty:** 2 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
Each trading day, futures positions are repriced to the settlement price; gains are credited and losses debited to margin accounts. This converts a multi-period forward exposure into a series of one-day settlements, eliminating credit risk buildup but introducing reinvestment/cash-flow timing risk.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Daily P&L = (Settle_t − Settle_{t-1}) × contract size × N

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Forgetting that daily MTM creates cash-flow risk even when terminal P&L is the same
- Confusing MTM cash flows with the convergence value at expiration

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- AnalystPrep CFA L1 — Derivatives Question Bank

**LOS:** LOS 2.d

# Maintenance margin (futures)

**LOS:** LOS 2.d
**Difficulty:** 2 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
Maintenance margin is the minimum balance the margin account must hold; if the balance falls below this floor, the exchange issues a margin call requiring restoration to the initial margin level (not just maintenance).

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- —

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Restoring to maintenance level instead of initial margin after a call — incorrect
- Confusing futures maintenance with equity maintenance margin (different mechanics)

## Suggested resources

children.notes:
# Variation margin

**LOS:** LOS 2.d
**Difficulty:** 2 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
Variation margin is the cash transfer required to top a margin account back to the initial margin level after a margin call (or to settle daily MTM gains/losses in cleared OTC contexts). The size equals the deficit, not just the breach.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Variation margin = Initial margin − Current balance (after call)

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Posting only the gap to maintenance instead of restoring to initial margin

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives

children.title: Basis (cash − futures)
children.notes:
# Basis (cash − futures)

**LOS:** LOS 2.e
**Difficulty:** 2 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
Basis is the difference between the spot price of the underlying and the futures price (Spot − Futures). Basis converges to zero at expiration; before expiration, basis reflects cost of carry (financing minus convenience yield/income). Hedgers face basis risk when the contract underlying differs from the position being hedged.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Basis = Spot − Futures; at T: Basis_T = 0

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Sign convention errors — some texts define basis as Futures − Spot
- Ignoring basis risk in cross-hedges (e.g. WTI futures hedging Brent exposure)

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- AnalystPrep CFA L1 — Derivatives Question Bank

children.notes:
# Contango

**LOS:** LOS 2.e
**Difficulty:** 2 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
Contango describes a futures market where the futures price exceeds the spot price (or longer-maturity futures exceed shorter-maturity). Typical for storable commodities with low convenience yield — financing and storage costs dominate.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Contango: F0 > S0

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Confusing contango (price relationship) with normal contango (futures > expected future spot)

## Suggested resources

children.notes:
# Backwardation

**LOS:** LOS 2.e
**Difficulty:** 2 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
Backwardation describes a market where futures price is below spot (F0 < S0) or longer-maturity futures are below shorter-maturity. Indicates high convenience yield (immediate scarcity) — typical in tight oil markets or supply shocks.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Backwardation: F0 < S0

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Mixing up backwardation with normal backwardation (futures < expected future spot)

## Suggested resources

children.title: Open interest and convergence at expiration
children.notes:
# Open interest and convergence at expiration

**LOS:** LOS 2.d
**Difficulty:** 2 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
Open interest is the total number of outstanding (not yet closed or delivered) futures contracts; rises with new positions, falls as positions are closed. At expiration the futures price converges to the spot price (basis → 0), enforced by arbitrage — otherwise an immediate cash-and-carry trade would lock in profit.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- At T: F_T = S_T (convergence)

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Confusing open interest with trading volume
- Forgetting that convergence is enforced by arbitrage, not by exchange rule

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- AnalystPrep CFA L1 — Derivatives Question Bank

children.title: Equity index futures
children.notes:
# Equity index futures

**LOS:** LOS 2.f
**Difficulty:** 2 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
Equity index futures (e.g. E-mini S&P 500) settle in cash to a final index value; multiplier converts index points to dollar value (e.g. $50 × index). Used for hedging equity portfolios, beta adjustment, and synthetic equity exposure with low capital.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Notional = Index level × multiplier × contracts; Beta hedge: N = (β_target − β_port) × V_port / (β_fut × P_fut × multiplier)

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Forgetting the multiplier when computing dollar P&L
- Using the wrong futures beta (typically assumed 1.0 for the index itself)

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- AnalystPrep CFA L1 — Derivatives Question Bank

# Interest rate futures

**LOS:** LOS 2.f
**Difficulty:** 3 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
Interest rate futures include short-term (e.g. 3-month SOFR/Eurodollar) priced as 100 − rate, and Treasury bond/note futures with cheapest-to-deliver mechanics. Tick value is fixed by the contract spec; PVBP-based hedge ratios convert duration exposure to contract counts.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- SOFR futures price = 100 − implied rate; Hedge contracts ≈ (PVBP_portfolio / PVBP_CTD) × CF

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Confusing 100 − rate quote with the actual P&L (1 bp ≈ $25 on a 3-month $1mm contract)
- Ignoring the conversion factor on bond futures

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives

**LOS:** LOS 2.f
**Difficulty:** 2 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

children.notes:
# Commodity futures

**LOS:** LOS 2.f
**Difficulty:** 2 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
Commodity futures price the cost of carry: financing cost plus storage cost minus convenience yield (and any income). Roll yield (positive in backwardation, negative in contango) drives long-term commodity index returns more than spot moves.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- F0 = S0 × e^((r + storage − convenience)×T)

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Ignoring roll yield when assessing long-only commodity index returns
- Confusing convenience yield (intangible) with lease rate (precious metals)

children.title: Forward pricing — no-arbitrage / cash-and-carry
children.notes:
# Forward pricing — no-arbitrage / cash-and-carry

**LOS:** LOS 3.a
**Difficulty:** 3 · **Estimated:** 0.5h

## Opis
The no-arbitrage forward price equals the future value of spot adjusted for income/yield and any storage cost. For an asset with no income: F0 = S0 × (1+r)^T. For an asset with income (dividends, coupons): F0 = (S0 − PV(income)) × (1+r)^T or F0 = S0 × (1+r)^T − FV(income). Violations of this relationship enable cash-and-carry or reverse cash-and-carry arbitrage.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- F0 = (S0 − PV(I)) × (1+r)^T (discrete); F0 = S0 × e^((r − q)×T) (continuous, yield q)

## Prerequisites
- Time value of money
- Spot rates

## Common pitfalls
- Forgetting to subtract PV of income (dividends, coupons) before compounding
- Mixing discrete and continuous compounding within a single problem

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- AnalystPrep CFA L1 — Derivatives Question Bank

Mixing discrete and continuous compounding within a single problem

children.title: Valuation of a forward between dates
children.notes:
# Valuation of a forward between dates

**LOS:** LOS 3.b
**Difficulty:** 4 · **Estimated:** 0.5h

## Opis
After inception the forward has nonzero value reflecting drift in spot relative to the contracted F0. For a long forward at time t (no income): Vt(long) = St − F0/(1+r)^(T−t). At inception V0 = 0; at maturity VT = ST − F0. Direction matters — value to short is the negative.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Vt(long, no income) = St − F0 × (1+r)^(−(T−t)); Vt(long, with yield q) = St × e^(−q(T−t)) − F0 × e^(−r(T−t))

## Prerequisites
- Time value of money

## Common pitfalls
- Discounting F0 over the wrong period (use remaining life T−t, not original T)
- Mixing up sign for short side
- Forgetting income adjustment when valuing during life

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- Hull, "Options, Futures, and Other Derivatives" (reference, advanced)

children.title: Cost-of-carry framework
children.notes:
# Cost-of-carry framework

**LOS:** LOS 3.a
**Difficulty:** 3 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
Cost of carry is the net cost of holding the underlying to delivery: financing rate plus storage minus income/convenience. The forward price equals spot grown at the cost of carry. This unifies pricing across asset classes (equities pay dividends, bonds pay coupons, currencies earn foreign rate, commodities have storage and convenience).

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- F0 = S0 × (1 + cost of carry)^T

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Omitting any cost-of-carry component (storage, convenience, dividend) for asset class
- Sign confusion — income/convenience reduces forward, storage/financing increases it

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- AnalystPrep CFA L1 — Derivatives Question Bank

children.title: Forward pricing for an underlying with varying cash flows
children.notes:
# Forward pricing for an underlying with varying cash flows

**LOS:** LOS 3.c
**Difficulty:** 3 · **Estimated:** 0.5h

## Opis
When the underlying generates discrete known cash flows during contract life (coupon bond, dividend-paying stock with known schedule), subtract PV of those flows from spot before compounding to F0. For continuous yield q (broad equity index, foreign currency): F0 = S0 × e^((r−q)T).

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- F0 = (S0 − Σ PV(CFi)) × (1+r)^T

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Including cash flows that occur after T
- Discounting at wrong rate (use risk-free, not stock yield)

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- AnalystPrep CFA L1 — Derivatives Question Bank

children.title: Pricing and valuation of futures contracts
children.notes:
# Pricing and valuation of futures contracts

**LOS:** LOS 3.d
**Difficulty:** 3 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
Under deterministic interest rates, futures prices equal forward prices. Under stochastic rates, daily MTM creates a small wedge (futures > forward when rates are positively correlated with the underlying — Eurodollar-style; opposite for negative correlation). At L1 the convention is to treat F_futures = F_forward.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- F_futures ≈ F_forward (deterministic rates); under stochastic rates there is a convexity adjustment

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Stating that futures and forwards have identical value during life — they differ because of MTM cash flows
- Forgetting the convexity adjustment exists for long-dated rate futures

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- Hull, "Options, Futures, and Other Derivatives" (reference, advanced)

children.title: FRA mechanics
children.notes:
# FRA mechanics

**LOS:** LOS 3.e
**Difficulty:** 3 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
A forward rate agreement (FRA) is an OTC contract locking in a future borrowing/lending rate on a notional principal. The "1×4" FRA starts in 1 month, references a 3-month rate (4-month minus 1-month). The buyer (long FRA) profits if realized rate exceeds FRA rate.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- FRA notation X×Y: starts in X months, ends in Y months, references (Y−X)-month rate

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Misreading the X×Y notation — the difference (Y−X) is the underlying tenor
- Confusing buyer (pays fixed, receives floating) with seller

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- AnalystPrep CFA L1 — Derivatives Question Bank

children.title: FRA pricing
children.notes:
# FRA pricing

**LOS:** LOS 3.e
**Difficulty:** 3 · **Estimated:** 0.5h

## Opis
The fair FRA rate is the implied forward rate from the term structure that makes initial value zero. Derived from spot rates such that investing at the long spot equals investing at the short spot rolled at the FRA rate.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- (1 + R_long × t_long) = (1 + R_short × t_short) × (1 + FRA × t_FRA)

## Prerequisites
- Spot rates
- Forward rates

## Common pitfalls
- Mixing day-count fractions (actual/360 vs 30/360) between currencies
- Using compound when contract is on simple/money-market basis

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- AnalystPrep CFA L1 — Derivatives Question Bank

children.title: FRA settlement formula
children.notes:
# FRA settlement formula

**LOS:** LOS 3.e
**Difficulty:** 3 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
FRAs settle in advance: at the start of the underlying period, the difference between the realized reference rate and the FRA rate is computed, applied to notional, and discounted back to settlement date at the realized rate.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Payment to long = Notional × (R_realized − R_FRA) × (days/360) / (1 + R_realized × days/360)

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Forgetting the discount-to-settlement step (FRAs settle at start, not end of period)
- Using the FRA rate to discount instead of the realized rate

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- AnalystPrep CFA L1 — Derivatives Question Bank

children.title: Plain vanilla interest rate swap (fixed-for-floating)
children.notes:
# Plain vanilla interest rate swap (fixed-for-floating)

**LOS:** LOS 4.a
**Difficulty:** 3 · **Estimated:** 0.5h

## Opis
A plain vanilla swap exchanges fixed-rate payments for floating-rate payments (e.g. SOFR) on the same notional, with payment dates and tenor agreed at inception. The fixed rate (swap rate) is set so the swap has zero value at inception. Used to convert floating-rate debt to synthetic fixed (pay fixed, receive floating) or vice versa.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Net payment to fixed payer = Notional × (Floating − Fixed) × (days/360)

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Confusing pay-fixed (long swap) with pay-floating side
- Forgetting that the floating rate for period n is set at the start of period n (in advance) but paid at end (in arrears)

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- AnalystPrep CFA L1 — Derivatives Question Bank

children.title: Currency swap
children.notes:
# Currency swap

**LOS:** LOS 4.a
**Difficulty:** 3 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
A currency swap exchanges principal and interest payments in two different currencies. Unlike interest rate swaps, principal IS exchanged at inception and re-exchanged at maturity, at the spot rate at inception. Common variants: fixed-for-fixed, fixed-for-floating, floating-for-floating cross-currency.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Notional_A × initial spot = Notional_B (at inception)

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Forgetting principal exchanges at start and end (unlike IRS)
- Using current spot to exchange final principal — uses the inception spot

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- AnalystPrep CFA L1 — Derivatives Question Bank

children.title: Equity swap
children.notes:
# Equity swap

**LOS:** LOS 4.a
**Difficulty:** 3 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
An equity swap pays the total return on an equity (or index) in exchange for fixed, floating, or another equity's return. Used for synthetic equity exposure (no need to hold cash position), tax/regulatory arbitrage, and large block exposure changes without market impact.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Net to equity-receiver = (Equity total return − Reference rate) × Notional

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Forgetting that dividends flow through to the equity-return receiver
- Confusing equity swap with TRS on a single bond

children.notes:
# Swap as a portfolio of FRAs

**LOS:** LOS 4.b
**Difficulty:** 3 · **Estimated:** 0.5h

## Opis
A pay-fixed/receive-float swap is equivalent to a portfolio of long FRAs at each payment date — each leg locks a future-period borrowing rate. Conceptually useful for valuing the swap as the sum of FRA values across all reset dates.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- V_swap (pay fixed) = Σ V_FRA(reset_i)

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Treating the swap as a single instrument when bootstrapping for valuation
- Forgetting that the first FRA is the swap's next payment, not a hypothetical contract

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives

children.title: Swap as a pair of bonds
children.notes:
# Swap as a pair of bonds

**LOS:** LOS 4.b
**Difficulty:** 3 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
A pay-fixed swap is equivalent to being long a floating-rate bond and short a fixed-rate bond (both at par at inception). At each payment date the long FRN receives floating; short fixed-rate bond pays fixed. Initial value zero because both bonds price at par.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- V_swap (pay fixed) = B_floating − B_fixed; at inception both = par → V0 = 0

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Forgetting that the floating bond resets to par at each reset date — simplifies valuation
- Using market YTM instead of the original swap fixed rate to value the fixed leg

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- Hull, "Options, Futures, and Other Derivatives" (reference, advanced)

children.title: Swap pricing at inception (par swap rate)
children.notes:
# Swap pricing at inception (par swap rate)

**LOS:** LOS 4.c
**Difficulty:** 4 · **Estimated:** 0.5h

## Opis
The par swap rate makes the present value of fixed leg equal to the present value of expected floating leg, so the swap has zero initial value. Solved from the spot/discount curve: equates 1 minus terminal discount factor to the fixed coupon × sum of discount factors.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Swap rate = (1 − Z_n) / Σ Z_i  (Z_i = discount factor at payment date i)

## Prerequisites
- Spot rates
- Bootstrapping

## Common pitfalls
- Using current par yield as the swap rate ignores discount factor structure
- Forgetting day-count adjustments per leg

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- Hull, "Options, Futures, and Other Derivatives" (reference, advanced)

formulas: Swap rate = (1 − Z_n) / Σ Z_i  (Z_i = discount factor at payment date i)
common_pitfalls: Using current par yield as the swap rate ignores discount factor structure
Forgetting day-count adjustments per leg
suggested_resources: CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives

children.title: Swap valuation during life
children.notes:
# Swap valuation during life

**LOS:** LOS 4.d
**Difficulty:** 4 · **Estimated:** 1h

## Opis
After inception, swap value drifts as the term structure moves. Re-solve for the new par swap rate from current discount factors, then value the existing swap as the present value of the difference between original and new fixed legs (multiplied by sum of remaining discount factors and notional). Equivalent to revaluing the implied bond pair.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- V_t (pay fixed) = (Swap_new − Swap_orig) × Σ Z_i × Notional × period

## Prerequisites
- Time value of money
- Spot rates

## Common pitfalls
- Updating one leg without the other
- Mishandling the in-period accrued interest on floating leg between resets

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- Hull, "Options, Futures, and Other Derivatives" (reference, advanced)

formulas: V_t (pay fixed) = (Swap_new − Swap_orig) × Σ Z_i × Notional × period
common_pitfalls: Updating one leg without the other
Mishandling the in-period accrued interest on floating leg between resets

children.title: Swap counterparty risk and clearing
children.notes:
# Swap counterparty risk and clearing

**LOS:** LOS 4.e
**Difficulty:** 2 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
Like forwards, OTC swaps expose counterparties to default risk. Post-2008 reforms require most standardized swaps to clear through central counterparties (CCPs) with daily margining (initial + variation). Bilateral uncleared swaps still trade under ISDA Master Agreement with CSAs requiring collateral.

children.title: Call option
children.notes:
# Call option

**LOS:** LOS 5.a
**Difficulty:** 1 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
A call option grants the holder the right (not obligation) to buy the underlying at strike X by/at expiration. Long call profits when ST > X + premium; short call has unlimited loss potential when ST rises far above X.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Call payoff at T = max(ST − X, 0); Call profit = max(ST − X, 0) − c0

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Confusing payoff (ignores premium) with profit (nets premium)
- Treating short call as bounded loss — it is unlimited above strike

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- AnalystPrep CFA L1 — Derivatives Question Bank

children.title: Put option
children.notes:
# Put option

**LOS:** LOS 5.a
**Difficulty:** 1 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
A put option grants the holder the right to sell the underlying at strike X by/at expiration. Long put profits when ST < X − premium; max gain capped at X (asset cannot go below zero). Short put has loss capped at X − premium per share.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Put payoff at T = max(X − ST, 0); Put profit = max(X − ST, 0) − p0

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Forgetting put has bounded max gain (X) — unlike call which is unbounded
- Reversing put-call payoff formulas under exam pressure

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- AnalystPrep CFA L1 — Derivatives Question Bank

children.notes:
# American option

**LOS:** LOS 5.b
**Difficulty:** 1 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
American options can be exercised at any time up to and including expiration. For a non-dividend-paying call this early-exercise feature is worth nothing extra (American call = European call); for puts and dividend-paying calls American value can exceed European.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- C_American ≥ C_European; for non-dividend stock, equality

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Assuming American calls always > European calls — for no-dividend stock, equality holds
- Forgetting that early exercise of an American put can be optimal

## Suggested resources

# Moneyness — in-the-money (ITM)

**LOS:** LOS 5.c
**Difficulty:** 1 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
A call is ITM when S > X (positive intrinsic value); a put is ITM when S < X. ITM options have positive intrinsic value and trade at a premium that is at least their intrinsic value.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Call ITM: S > X; Put ITM: S < X

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Reversing the inequality between call and put ITM conditions

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"

children.notes:
# Moneyness — at-the-money (ATM)

**LOS:** LOS 5.c
**Difficulty:** 1 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
An option is ATM when spot equals strike (S = X). ATM options have zero intrinsic value but maximum time value (gamma is highest near ATM at expiration).

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- ATM: S = X; intrinsic = 0

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Calling near-ATM options "in the money" — strict definition requires equality
- Forgetting that ATM options have the highest sensitivity to volatility (vega)

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives

children.notes:
# Moneyness — out-of-the-money (OTM)

**LOS:** LOS 5.c
**Difficulty:** 1 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
A call is OTM when S < X; a put is OTM when S > X. OTM options have zero intrinsic value; the entire premium is time value, which decays to zero as expiration approaches.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Call OTM: S < X; Put OTM: S > X

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Treating OTM options as worthless before expiration — they have time value
- Reversing call/put inequalities

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives

children.title: Intrinsic value of an option
children.notes:
# Intrinsic value of an option

**LOS:** LOS 5.d
**Difficulty:** 2 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
Intrinsic value is the payoff if exercised immediately: max(S−X, 0) for a call, max(X−S, 0) for a put. Cannot be negative; ITM options have positive intrinsic, OTM/ATM options have zero.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Intrinsic_call = max(S − X, 0); Intrinsic_put = max(X − S, 0)

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Allowing intrinsic value to be negative — it is floored at zero
- Confusing intrinsic value with time value

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- AnalystPrep CFA L1 — Derivatives Question Bank

children.title: Time value of an option
children.notes:
# Time value of an option

**LOS:** LOS 5.d
**Difficulty:** 2 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
Time value (also "speculative value") is option premium minus intrinsic value. Reflects the chance that further price moves before expiration push the option deeper ITM. Decays toward zero at expiration (theta), fastest near ATM in the final weeks.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Time value = Premium − Intrinsic value

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Confusing intrinsic vs time value
- Assuming time decay is linear — it accelerates close to expiration

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- AnalystPrep CFA L1 — Derivatives Question Bank

Assuming time decay is linear — it accelerates close to expiration

# Payoff and profit diagrams

Long/short call and put payoff diagrams plus the profit-vs-payoff distinction with break-evens.

children.title: Payoff diagram — long call
children.notes:
# Payoff diagram — long call

**LOS:** LOS 5.e
**Difficulty:** 2 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
Long call payoff at expiration: zero for ST ≤ X, then increases 1-for-1 above X. Plotted as a hockey stick with kink at strike. Profit diagram shifts the entire curve down by the premium paid.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Payoff = max(ST − X, 0); break-even = X + c0

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Plotting the curve including premium (this is profit, not payoff)
- Forgetting payoff is unbounded above strike

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- AnalystPrep CFA L1 — Derivatives Question Bank

children.notes:
# Payoff diagram — short call

**LOS:** LOS 5.e
**Difficulty:** 2 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
Short call payoff at expiration: zero for ST ≤ X, then decreases (becomes negative) 1-for-1 above X. Mirror image of long call across x-axis. Loss is unbounded; gain is capped at premium received.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Payoff = −max(ST − X, 0); break-even = X + c0

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Capping the loss — short call is unlimited downside above strike
- Reversing slope direction

## Suggested resources

# Payoff diagram — long put

**LOS:** LOS 5.e
**Difficulty:** 2 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
Long put payoff at expiration: increasing as ST drops below X (slope −1 to the left of X), zero at and above X. Maximum payoff equals X (when ST = 0). Profit diagram shifts down by premium.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Payoff = max(X − ST, 0); break-even = X − p0

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Forgetting put payoff has a maximum (X) — unlike call which is unbounded

# Payoff diagram — short put

**LOS:** LOS 5.e
**Difficulty:** 2 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
Short put payoff at expiration: negative when ST < X (loss equals X − ST), zero when ST ≥ X. Maximum loss equals X − p0 (if stock goes to zero). Profit capped at premium received.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Payoff = −max(X − ST, 0); max loss = X − p0

- —

- Treating short put loss as unlimited — bounded by strike
- Confusing short put profit/loss orientation

children.title: Profit diagram — netting the option premium
children.notes:
# Profit diagram — netting the option premium

**LOS:** LOS 5.e
**Difficulty:** 2 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
Profit diagrams shift the payoff diagram vertically by the option premium (down for long, up for short). Break-even points emerge: long call breaks even at X + c0; long put at X − p0; short call at X + c0; short put at X − p0. Critical for question framing distinguishing payoff (at expiration only) vs profit (after upfront premium).

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Profit_long = Payoff − Premium; Profit_short = Premium − |Payoff|

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Plotting payoff when the question asks for profit (and vice versa)
- Computing break-even using strike alone, ignoring the premium

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- AnalystPrep CFA L1 — Derivatives Question Bank

children.title: Put-call parity
children.notes:
# Put-call parity

**LOS:** LOS 5.f
**Difficulty:** 3 · **Estimated:** 0.5h

## Opis
For European options on a non-dividend asset: c0 + X/(1+r)^T = p0 + S0. Establishes equivalence between a fiduciary call (call + risk-free bond at PV of strike) and a protective put (put + stock). Used to derive synthetic positions and check arbitrage.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- c0 + X/(1+r)^T = p0 + S0  (European, no dividends)

## Prerequisites
- Time value of money

## Common pitfalls
- Applying to American options without adjustment
- Forgetting to adjust for dividends or carry costs

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- AnalystPrep CFA L1 — Derivatives Question Bank

children.title: Put-call-forward parity
children.notes:
# Put-call-forward parity

**LOS:** LOS 5.f
**Difficulty:** 3 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
When using a forward instead of spot: c0 − p0 = (F0 − X) / (1+r)^T. Useful when forward is the directly observable benchmark (FX, commodities) and avoids needing dividend yield estimates.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- c0 − p0 = (F0 − X) × (1+r)^(−T)

## Prerequisites
- Forward pricing

## Common pitfalls
- Using spot when forward is the cleaner reference
- Forgetting to discount the forward differential

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives

children.title: Lower bound on European call
children.notes:
# Lower bound on European call

**LOS:** LOS 5.g
**Difficulty:** 3 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
European call value cannot fall below max(0, S0 − X/(1+r)^T) — derived from no-arbitrage; if call < this bound, buying the call and shorting the underlying with appropriate financing locks in riskless profit.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- c0 ≥ max(0, S0 − X/(1+r)^T)

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Stating the lower bound as max(0, S0 − X) — that is the intrinsic, not the no-arbitrage lower bound
- Forgetting time value of money in discounting strike

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives

**LOS:** LOS 5.h
**Difficulty:** 3 · **Estimated:** 0.5h

## Opis
Sensitivities: rising S → call up, put down. Rising X → call down, put up. Rising T → both up (more time value, except deep-ITM European put on dividend stock). Rising r → call up, put down (PV of strike falls). Rising sigma → both up (greater dispersion). Rising dividends → call down, put up (dividends reduce expected ST).

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Directional table: S↑(c↑,p↓); X↑(c↓,p↑); sigma↑(c↑,p↑); r↑(c↑,p↓); div↑(c↓,p↑); T↑(c↑,p↑ usually)

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Reversing the rate effect (rate up reduces PV of strike, helps the call buyer)
- Saying T always increases European put — can decrease for deep-ITM European put on a dividend payer

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- AnalystPrep CFA L1 — Derivatives Question Bank

children.title: Binomial and Black-Scholes-Merton pricing
children.notes:
# Binomial and Black-Scholes-Merton pricing

One-period and multi-period binomial valuation, risk-neutral probability, and BSM inputs and assumptions.

**Learning Modules:** 1 · **Egzamin weight (topic):** 6.5%

children.title: One-period binomial pricing model
children.notes:
# One-period binomial pricing model

**LOS:** LOS 5.i
**Difficulty:** 3 · **Estimated:** 0.5h

## Opis
In a one-period binomial model the underlying moves up to S×u or down to S×d. The option is replicated by holding h shares of stock and borrowing B at the risk-free rate, where h = (c+ − c−)/(S×(u−d)). Today's option value equals h×S − B (or equivalently the risk-neutral expected payoff discounted).

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- c0 = (π × c+ + (1−π) × c−) / (1+r); π = (1+r−d)/(u−d)

## Prerequisites
- Time value of money

## Common pitfalls
- Using actual probabilities instead of risk-neutral π
- Mishandling the borrow amount (B) sign in the replicating portfolio

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- Hull, "Options, Futures, and Other Derivatives" (reference, advanced)

children.title: Multi-period binomial pricing model
children.notes:
# Multi-period binomial pricing model

**LOS:** LOS 5.i
**Difficulty:** 3 · **Estimated:** 0.5h

## Opis
Extends one-period model to a tree with N periods. Value the option by working backward from terminal nodes: at each node, value = (π × Vup + (1−π) × Vdown) / (1+r). For American options, compare continuation value with intrinsic at each node and take the max.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- V_node = max(intrinsic, (π Vup + (1−π) Vdown)/(1+r))  (American)

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Using arithmetic average of children instead of risk-neutral weights
- Skipping the early-exercise check at each node for American options

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- Hull, "Options, Futures, and Other Derivatives" (reference, advanced)

children.title: Risk-neutral probability
children.notes:
# Risk-neutral probability

**LOS:** LOS 5.j
**Difficulty:** 3 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
Risk-neutral probability π = (1+r−d)/(u−d) is the synthetic probability under which the discounted underlying price is a martingale. Used in derivative pricing to discount expected payoffs at the risk-free rate without needing to estimate true expected returns or risk premia.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- π = (1+r − d) / (u − d);  E_RN[ST]/(1+r)^T = S0

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Confusing risk-neutral π with the real-world probability of an up-move
- Forgetting that π must lie in (0,1) for no-arbitrage; otherwise model parameters are inconsistent

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- Hull, "Options, Futures, and Other Derivatives" (reference, advanced)

children.title: Black-Scholes-Merton inputs
children.notes:
# Black-Scholes-Merton inputs

**LOS:** LOS 5.k
**Difficulty:** 2 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
BSM at L1 is presented as a closed-form value depending on five observable inputs (spot S, strike X, time T, risk-free rate r, volatility sigma) plus one estimable input (continuous dividend yield q). Sigma is the only non-observable input — implied volatility is the value that equates BSM to market price.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- c = S × e^(−qT) × N(d1) − X × e^(−rT) × N(d2)  (formula not derived at L1)

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Forgetting sigma is the volatility of returns, annualized
- Confusing implied volatility (from market) with historical volatility

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- Hull, "Options, Futures, and Other Derivatives" (reference, advanced)

children.title: BSM assumptions
children.notes:
# BSM assumptions

**LOS:** LOS 5.k
**Difficulty:** 3 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
Key assumptions: underlying follows geometric Brownian motion with constant volatility, continuous trading and frictionless markets, constant risk-free rate, no transaction costs/taxes, European exercise, no arbitrage. Real markets violate constant-vol (smile/skew exists) and continuous trading (jumps); practitioners use the model with caveats.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- —

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Listing assumptions BSM does not require (e.g. risk-neutral world is not an assumption — it is a pricing technique)
- Forgetting BSM assumes European exercise — does not directly value American puts

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- Hull, "Options, Futures, and Other Derivatives" (reference, advanced)

# Option Strategies and Greeks

LM 6 covers the Greeks at L1 overview level (Delta, Gamma, Vega, Theta, Rho) and the canonical multi-leg option strategies: protective put, covered call, collar, long/short straddle and strangle, bull call spread, bear put spread, and butterfly.

**Learning Modules:** 1 · **Egzamin weight (topic):** 6.5%

children.title: Greeks — Delta, Gamma, Vega, Theta, Rho
children.notes:
# Greeks — Delta, Gamma, Vega, Theta, Rho

First- and second-order option sensitivities and their use in hedging.

**Learning Modules:** 1 · **Egzamin weight (topic):** 6.5%

children.title: Delta
children.notes:
# Delta

**LOS:** LOS 6.a
**Difficulty:** 3 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
Delta is the rate of change of option value with respect to the underlying price. Long call delta lies between 0 and 1; long put between −1 and 0. Used as a hedge ratio: a delta-neutral portfolio has zero first-order sensitivity to small underlying moves. Approximates risk-neutral probability of finishing ITM.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Delta_call = ∂c/∂S; ranges (0,1); Delta_put = ∂p/∂S; ranges (−1,0)

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Forgetting delta sign for puts (negative)
- Treating delta as a constant — it changes with S (gamma)

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- Hull, "Options, Futures, and Other Derivatives" (reference, advanced)

common_pitfalls: Forgetting delta sign for puts (negative)
Treating delta as a constant — it changes with S (gamma)

children.title: Gamma
children.notes:
# Gamma

**LOS:** LOS 6.a
**Difficulty:** 3 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
Gamma is the rate of change of delta with respect to the underlying — second derivative of option price w.r.t. S. Largest near ATM and approaching expiration. High gamma means delta-hedges need frequent rebalancing; long gamma profits from realized volatility.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Gamma = ∂²c/∂S² = ∂Delta/∂S; same sign for calls and puts (positive for long options)

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Believing gamma is highest deep ITM/OTM — it is highest ATM
- Forgetting both calls and puts have positive gamma when long

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- Hull, "Options, Futures, and Other Derivatives" (reference, advanced)

children.title: Vega
children.notes:
# Vega

**LOS:** LOS 6.a
**Difficulty:** 3 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
Vega measures sensitivity of option value to volatility (sigma). Always positive for long options (more volatility = higher value); largest for ATM options with longer time to expiration. Quoted as price change per 1 percentage point change in vol.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Vega = ∂c/∂sigma > 0 (long options); units: price change per 1% vol change

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Confusing vega with gamma — vega responds to vol changes, gamma responds to underlying changes
- Forgetting vega decays as expiration nears

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- Hull, "Options, Futures, and Other Derivatives" (reference, advanced)

children.title: Theta
children.notes:
# Theta

**LOS:** LOS 6.a
**Difficulty:** 3 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
Theta measures the rate of decay in option value with passage of time, holding all else constant. Generally negative for long options (time decay); accelerates close to expiration, especially for ATM options. Short option positions earn theta but bear gamma risk.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Theta = ∂c/∂t (with t increasing, value decays — typically reported as negative)

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Treating theta decay as linear — it accelerates near expiration
- Reversing sign convention (some texts report as negative loss per day; others as positive decay)

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- Hull, "Options, Futures, and Other Derivatives" (reference, advanced)

children.title: Rho
children.notes:
# Rho

**LOS:** LOS 6.a
**Difficulty:** 2 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
Rho measures sensitivity of option value to the risk-free rate. Positive for calls (higher rate → lower PV(X) → higher call value), negative for puts. Generally small relative to other Greeks for short-dated equity options; matters more for long-dated and FX options.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Rho_call = ∂c/∂r > 0; Rho_put = ∂p/∂r < 0

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Treating rho as immaterial for all options — meaningful for long-dated
- Reversing call/put sign

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- Hull, "Options, Futures, and Other Derivatives" (reference, advanced)

Reversing call/put sign

children.title: Multi-leg option strategies
children.notes:
# Multi-leg option strategies

Hedging strategies (protective put, covered call, collar) and directional/volatility spreads (straddle, strangle, vertical, butterfly).

**Learning Modules:** 1 · **Egzamin weight (topic):** 6.5%

children.title: Protective put
children.notes:
# Protective put

**LOS:** LOS 6.b
**Difficulty:** 2 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
Protective put = long stock + long put. Caps downside at the strike (less premium) while preserving full upside (less premium). Used as portfolio insurance; profit profile mirrors a long call (synthetic call via put-call parity).

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Profit = max(ST, X) − S0 − p0

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Confusing protective put profit with a covered call
- Forgetting that the floor is X − p0, not X

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- AnalystPrep CFA L1 — Derivatives Question Bank

common_pitfalls: Confusing protective put profit with a covered call
Forgetting that the floor is X − p0, not X

children.title: Covered call
children.notes:
# Covered call

**LOS:** LOS 6.b
**Difficulty:** 2 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
Covered call = long stock + short call. Limits upside to strike (plus premium) in exchange for the call premium received. Used to generate income from a held position; downside is unprotected (just less by the premium).

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Profit = min(ST, X) − S0 + c0

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Calling a covered call "low risk" — downside is not hedged
- Forgetting that the cap is X + c0, not X

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- AnalystPrep CFA L1 — Derivatives Question Bank

Forgetting that the cap is X + c0, not X

children.title: Collar
children.notes:
# Collar

**LOS:** LOS 6.b
**Difficulty:** 3 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
Collar = long stock + long put (lower strike Xp) + short call (higher strike Xc). The short call premium funds (partially or fully) the long put premium. Caps both upside (at Xc) and downside (at Xp) — a costless collar uses Xp and Xc that exactly equate premiums.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Profit = max(min(ST, Xc), Xp) − S0 + c0 − p0

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Forgetting both strikes constrain the payoff (asymmetric)
- Treating "costless" as zero-risk — risk remains in the price band

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- AnalystPrep CFA L1 — Derivatives Question Bank

children.title: Long straddle
children.notes:
# Long straddle

**LOS:** LOS 6.c
**Difficulty:** 3 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
Long straddle = long call + long put at the same strike (typically ATM) and same expiration. Profits from a large move in either direction; loses if the underlying stays near strike. Bet on volatility (long vega), not direction.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Profit = max(ST − X, 0) + max(X − ST, 0) − c0 − p0; break-evens X ± (c0+p0)

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Believing straddle pays off on small moves — it requires |ST − X| > c0 + p0
- Forgetting time decay erodes the position

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- AnalystPrep CFA L1 — Derivatives Question Bank

children.title: Long strangle
children.notes:
# Long strangle

**LOS:** LOS 6.c
**Difficulty:** 3 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
Long strangle = long call (strike Xc) + long put (strike Xp), with Xp < Xc (both OTM). Cheaper than a straddle but requires larger move to profit. Same volatility-bet motivation as a straddle.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Profit = max(ST − Xc, 0) + max(Xp − ST, 0) − c0 − p0

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Confusing strangle with straddle — strangle uses different strikes
- Choosing strikes too far OTM, requiring an unrealistic move

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- AnalystPrep CFA L1 — Derivatives Question Bank

children.title: Short straddle
children.notes:
# Short straddle

**LOS:** LOS 6.c
**Difficulty:** 3 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
Short straddle = short call + short put at the same strike. Profits if the underlying stays near strike (capture both premiums); unlimited loss if a large move occurs in either direction. Short volatility (short vega, short gamma).

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Max profit = c0 + p0 (if ST = X); loss unbounded

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Treating short straddle as low-risk income — tail risk is large
- Confusing payoff with profit

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- AnalystPrep CFA L1 — Derivatives Question Bank

children.title: Short strangle
children.notes:
# Short strangle

**LOS:** LOS 6.c
**Difficulty:** 3 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
Short strangle = short call (Xc) + short put (Xp), Xp < Xc. Wider profit zone than a short straddle but smaller premium. Still has unbounded loss outside the strike band.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Max profit = c0 + p0 (if Xp ≤ ST ≤ Xc); loss unbounded outside the band

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Underestimating tail risk because the profit band is wider
- Confusing strangle and straddle short positions

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- AnalystPrep CFA L1 — Derivatives Question Bank

children.title: Bull call spread
children.notes:
# Bull call spread

**LOS:** LOS 6.c
**Difficulty:** 3 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
Bull call spread = long call (lower strike X1) + short call (higher strike X2), X1 < X2. Bullish view with capped upside (X2 − X1) and capped loss (net premium). Lower cost than outright long call.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Profit = max(0, ST − X1) − max(0, ST − X2) − (c1 − c2); max profit = (X2 − X1) − net premium

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Forgetting net premium is positive (cost) — bull call spread is debit
- Mislabeling profit/loss caps

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- AnalystPrep CFA L1 — Derivatives Question Bank

Mislabeling profit/loss caps

children.title: Bear put spread
children.notes:
# Bear put spread

**LOS:** LOS 6.c
**Difficulty:** 3 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
Bear put spread = long put (higher strike X2) + short put (lower strike X1), X1 < X2. Bearish view with capped downside profit (X2 − X1) and capped loss (net premium). Lower cost than outright long put.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Profit = max(0, X2 − ST) − max(0, X1 − ST) − (p2 − p1); max profit = (X2 − X1) − net premium

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Confusing bear put spread (long higher strike) with bear call spread
- Mislabeling which leg is long

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- AnalystPrep CFA L1 — Derivatives Question Bank

children.title: Butterfly spread
children.notes:
# Butterfly spread

**LOS:** LOS 6.c
**Difficulty:** 3 · **Estimated:** 0.5h

## Opis
Long butterfly (calls): long 1 call at X1 + short 2 calls at X2 + long 1 call at X3, with X1 < X2 < X3 and X2 = (X1+X3)/2. Maximum profit at ST = X2 (the body strike) equals X2 − X1 − net premium; max loss = net premium. Bet on low realized volatility.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Profit max = (X2 − X1) − net premium (at ST = X2); max loss = net premium

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Mismatching the wing/body counts (1-2-1)
- Forgetting strikes must be equally spaced for a symmetric butterfly

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- Hull, "Options, Futures, and Other Derivatives" (reference, advanced)

children.title: Credit Default Swap (CDS) mechanics
children.notes:
# Credit Default Swap (CDS) mechanics

**LOS:** LOS 7.a
**Difficulty:** 3 · **Estimated:** 0.5h

## Opis
A CDS is a bilateral OTC contract where the protection buyer pays a periodic premium (the spread) to the protection seller in exchange for a contingent payment if a credit event occurs on a reference entity. Since post-2009 standardization, fixed coupons (100/500 bps) are used with upfront payments to balance market value. Cleared through CCPs for index trades.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- CDS upfront ≈ (Spread − Coupon) × Duration × Notional

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Treating CDS as insurance — buyer need not own the underlying bond
- Forgetting cleared trades use fixed coupons with upfront adjustment

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- AnalystPrep CFA L1 — Derivatives Question Bank

children.notes:
# Single-name CDS

**LOS:** LOS 7.a
**Difficulty:** 2 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
A single-name CDS references one specific entity (corporate or sovereign). Trigger events typically include bankruptcy, failure to pay, and (for non-US contracts) restructuring. Pricing reflects the entity's credit spread term structure.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- —

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Confusing trigger events across geographies (restructuring excluded in NA standard contracts)
- Treating single-name CDS as offsetting bond exposure perfectly — basis between CDS and bond spread can drift

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- AnalystPrep CFA L1 — Derivatives Question Bank

children.notes:
# Index CDS

**LOS:** LOS 7.a
**Difficulty:** 2 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
Index CDS (e.g. CDX.NA.IG, iTraxx Europe) reference a basket of names with equal weights. Provide diversified credit exposure with high liquidity; rolled every six months to refresh constituents. Settlement occurs proportionally on triggered names.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- —

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Treating an index CDS as a single CDS — multiple credits can trigger independently
- Forgetting roll-date risk and liquidity differences between on-the-run and off-the-run series

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- AnalystPrep CFA L1 — Derivatives Question Bank

children.title: CDS payoff and settlement
children.notes:
# CDS payoff and settlement

**LOS:** LOS 7.b
**Difficulty:** 3 · **Estimated:** 0.5h

## Opis
On a credit event the protection buyer is made whole on loss given default. Cash settlement uses an auction-determined recovery rate: payoff = Notional × (1 − Recovery). Physical settlement (now rare) involves delivering deliverable obligations against full notional.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Cash settlement payoff = Notional × (1 − Recovery rate)

## Prerequisites
- Loss given default
- Recovery rate

## Common pitfalls
- Using par value instead of notional
- Confusing recovery rate (post-event) with assumed recovery in pricing

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- AnalystPrep CFA L1 — Derivatives Question Bank

children.notes:

children.title: Cash-and-carry arbitrage
children.notes:
# Cash-and-carry arbitrage

**LOS:** LOS 7.c
**Difficulty:** 3 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
When the futures price exceeds the no-arbitrage forward (F0 > S0×(1+r)^T), arbitrageurs buy spot, finance with borrowing, and sell futures — locking in the discrepancy. Trades push spot up and futures down, restoring equilibrium.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Arb profit = F0 − S0 × (1+r)^T (if positive)

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Ignoring transaction and storage costs that may erode small arb spreads
- Forgetting income from underlying must be netted

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- Hull, "Options, Futures, and Other Derivatives" (reference, advanced)

children.title: Reverse cash-and-carry arbitrage
children.notes:
# Reverse cash-and-carry arbitrage

**LOS:** LOS 7.c
**Difficulty:** 3 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
When futures is too low (F0 < S0×(1+r)^T), short the underlying, invest proceeds at the risk-free rate, and buy futures. At delivery, take the underlying via futures and cover the short. Constrained by short-selling difficulty for some commodities.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Arb profit = S0 × (1+r)^T − F0 (if positive)

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Assuming all underlyings are easily shorted — physical commodities often are not
- Forgetting the dividend/income obligation when shorting

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- Hull, "Options, Futures, and Other Derivatives" (reference, advanced)

children.title: Implied repo rate
children.notes:
# Implied repo rate

**LOS:** LOS 7.c
**Difficulty:** 3 · **Estimated:** 0.25h

## Opis
The implied repo rate is the financing rate at which cash-and-carry yields zero profit — the breakeven rate solving F0 = S0×(1+r)^T for r. Compared to actual repo financing: if implied > actual, cash-and-carry is profitable; if implied < actual, reverse cash-and-carry is profitable.

## Formuły / kluczowe relacje
- Implied repo = (F0/S0)^(1/T) − 1

## Prerequisites
- —

## Common pitfalls
- Mixing simple vs continuous compounding
- Forgetting income on the underlying when computing implied repo on a coupon-paying bond

## Suggested resources
- CFA Institute Curriculum 2026, Volume 5, Reading "Derivatives"
- Schweser SchweserNotes Book 4, Study Session — Derivatives
- Hull, "Options, Futures, and Other Derivatives" (reference, advanced)
